replies.author: @goose7574
replies.text:
​@BrianBarbare thank you for your kind words. She really ticked me off, because Dementia/Alzheimer patients are some of the most vulnerable patients. I chose to personally care for my Mother and even lived with her for a little over 2 years (with my home just sitting), when she needed 24 hour care. She was such an incredible woman/mom/wife/etc., and so it was the least I could do. It was the hardest thing I've ever done in my nearly 45 years of life, but I would do it all over again. She actually passed away at my home (I brought her to my home a couple months before she passed, because it was easier, since she was living in a smaller condo and things like bathing and toileting her were tough with 2 people in such a small place). She ended up passing away in my bedroom, in her hospital bed, which was set up right next to my bed. I had never seen a person dying, or dead or anything like that, but when it's family, it's kind of amazing what your body and mind are able to get through. I'm now trying to get myself back to normal, because I've found out the hard way that it's very difficult on your body when you go 2 and a half years, only sleeping a few hours each night. So my sleep schedule has been rough. My body doesn't want to sleep past 3 hours and then I wake up like I slept 6-8hrs. I just need to be patient with myself...lol
Sorry for such a long comment. Thank you again for the kind words. 
Take Care!
🩷🙏🏻🩷

replies.author: @fishfood8719
replies.text: My mother ( a widow since 2000) died just before the holidays last year, her 92nd birthday would have been a week before Christmas.  2025 was my first Thanksgiving,  Christmas and her birthday without her. I retired just before 2020 from my job of 33 years earlier than planned as I could see her decline. By 2022 I was able to get her dementia diagnosed.  I was caring for her at home, but eventually hired otside services to assist also, until early in 2024  when she fell and broke her hip and I had to place her into a memory care facility which within months she also began receiving hospice care as she was never able to regain the ability to walk. I visted her at her assisted living almost every day and interacted with many of the other residents there as many were never or rarely visited by family. I can't describe the anger I felt when first hearing this story from another source, and the return of the anger when seeing it posted again. This f×#$!@! demon thinking that confusing and terrifying dementia patients like my mother, while also risking injury to their very frail bodies for a political stunt or Tiktok clout makes me think I might be capable of doing terrible things myself....towards her.
